summary: Cached supply-chain artifact generation, shared-model outputs, and release attestation wiring were implemented while accepting the SBOM/OpenVEX review changes.

observations.problem: The phony Make chain for openvex-draft reruns sbom-export-mapping and cyclonedx-sbom each time, and those generators repeat release/public manifest traversal before producing the final OpenVEX artifact.
observations.why_it_matters: The reports are deterministic, but repeated traversal makes local and CI supply-chain checks noticeably slower on large or Windows-backed workspaces.
observations.automation_candidate: Introduce file-backed generated targets or a shared in-process supply-chain report pipeline that reuses the already-written provenance, mapping, and CycloneDX artifacts when their dependency inputs have not changed.
observations.suggested_followup: The cached path now exists and emits the broader artifact family. The remaining operational decision is whether the new release attestation outputs should graduate from optional workflow evidence to a default hard release gate after enough soak time.
